feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Modernize SAP PI/PO Landscapes with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Modernization conversations are happening everywhere right now.  And honestly, many customers are asking the same question: “How do we move from SAP Process Integration / SAP Process Orchestration without disrupting what already works?”

That’s exactly what this upcoming partner webinar is built for.

We’ll walk through the latest migration and assessment tool updates, practical modernization strategies, and SAP’s long-term integration vision.  Plus, you’ll get guidance, resources, and real-world best practices to help customers move confidently to SAP Integration Suite.

The thing is that migration projects are rarely just technical upgrades.  They’re business continuity projects.  The more prepared you are, the easier those customer conversations become.

If you already have a basic understanding of SAP Process Integration / SAP Process Orchestration and SAP Integration Suite, this session will give you practical insights you can apply immediately.

Register now and join us.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/dec/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012360.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPIntegrationSuite #DigitalTransformation #EnterpriseIntegration

entry.title: Service Bindings with OAuth 2.0 for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
entry.content:
This post outlines the procedure to specify client credentials required for SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems to communicate with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, using OAuth 2.0 client authentication. OAuth 2.0 provides a secure, token-based approach that is required for newer integration scenarios with SAP BTP services.

Prerequisites

Before starting the procedure, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • Initial setup in SAP BTP has been performed manually or via the Set Up an Account for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition booster
  • Required country/region-specific processes are active for your subaccount.
  • SAP Note 3540443 – Electronic Communication Integration Framework: Implementation Overview has been implemented.
  • You have the Space Developer or Org Manager role in your SAP BTP subaccount.

Note: If initial setup for a process or business system is already complete, it does not need to be repeated for additional processes in the same subaccount region.

Step 1: Create the Service Binding

    • Navigate to the Subaccount — In the SAP BTP cockpit, go to the subaccount where the service binding will be created.

       

    • Access Instances and Subscriptions: Choose Services > Instances and Subscriptions.

 

    • Select Service Instance: Select the service instance created during the initial setup of the cloud edition.

 

    • Create Service Binding: In the Service Bindings section on the right, choose Create and fill in the fields as follows:

 

  • Finalize: Choose Create.

Special Instructions for France (E-Invoices and E-Reports)

If integrating to activate the E-Invoices and E-Reports (France) process, repeat the steps above but select 'Peppol Exchange' as the process instead of 'Other Processes' in the binding configuration.

 

Step 2: Retrieve the OAuth 2.0 Credentials

After creating the service binding, you need to retrieve the credentials to configure the OAuth 2.0 client in your SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system.

    • Access Instances and Subscriptions — In the SAP BTP cockpit, in the navigation area, choose Services > Instances and Subscriptions.

 

    • Select Service Instance — Under Instances, choose the service instance of the cloud edition. The details screen for the service instance expands on the right side.

 

    • View Credentials — In the details screen, choose a service binding name to open the Credentials screen.

 

  • Retrieve Details — The Credentials screen displays the url, clientId, clientSecret, and drccApiUrl for this service binding.

     

Note: You need these credentials in the next steps when configuring the OAuth 2.0 client.

Summary
You have now successfully created a service binding using OAuth 2.0 client authentication in SAP BTP and retrieved the credentials needed for the integration. The next step is to use the clientId, clientSecret, url, and drccApiUrl to configure the OAuth 2.0 client in your SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system, which will enable secure communication with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

entry.title: Shape What’s Next: SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026 Is Now Open
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that Cycle 2 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now live!
This cycle features 38 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to gain early insights into upcoming product developments and collaborate directly with SAP teams to shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

🗓Registration is open until June 19, 2026.

In a rapidly evolving business environment, early involvement can make a meaningful difference. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can work directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing your feedback on concepts, prototypes, and plans while they are still in the early stages.

To help you explore all available opportunities, we’ve prepared a complete overview of the projects.
View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the topics most relevant to you.

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-Assisted Error Remediation for SAP Application Interface Framework
  • AI assisted guidance in the UI theme designer
  • BTP Specific Data Privacy & AI based Data Masking
  • Evolution of SAP BTP Runtimes
  • Fulfillment of business needs through Business Adaption Plans and Solution Implementation
  • Improving the Experience on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime
  • Partner Perspectives in SAP BTP and AI development

Cloud ERP

  • Accelerate Integrations with AI powered SAP Integration Suite
  • Agentic AI in SAP S/4HANA Procurement (Private Cloud Edition)
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
  • AI-Powered Warranty Verification – Co-Innovate the future of service, repair & claims
  • Finance Intelligence on SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Intelligent Returns: Process Discovery & Automation Roadmap
  • Modernization Approaches in Purchase Order (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)
  • Parallel Unit of Measure

SAP Concur

  • Concur Invoice - AI-first, Reimagine Invoice Experience
  • Seamless configuration promotion for SAP Concur

SAP Globalization

  • AI for UI: Exploring the Usage of AI in Translating SAP's Product User Interface
  • Assessing Demand for AI Speech Services in SAP Translation Hub
  • PayTo Decoded: Let's Build It Together

Customer Success & Delivery

  • Improve SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis for SAP BTP and Cloud

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Cloud Global Landscape Management
  • SAP HANA Cloud Intelligent Advisors
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

Lifecycle Management

  • Comprehensive Planning Assistant for Maintenance Planner
  • New version of SAP Add-On Assembly Kit

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative meets SAP Integration Experience Feedback

  • Hands-on: AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Mgmt - Provisioning & Setup
  • Hands-on Experience of AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Management
  • Hands-on System Experience of the AI-Assisted Process Modeler in SAP Signavio

SAP Learning

  • Reimagining SAP Certification: Evolving a Skills-Based and Future-Ready Certification Experience

Others

  • Autonomous Supply Chain: Embodied AI in the Warehouse
  • Embodied AI Autonomous Asset Inspection
  • Embodied AI: Autonomous Visual Inspection for Quality and Safety
  • Iterate together on the User Experience for Joule Work
  • Providing feedback on the Discovery domain in SAP LeanIX
  • SAP Traceability Hub Repository
  • Support in Flow – Shaping Built-In Support Experience

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Unpacking SAP Business Data Cloud for Solution Architects
entry.content:
Every customer conversation today seems to come back to the same question: “How do we unify data for AI without creating more complexity?”

Honestly, that’s exactly why this session matters.

In our upcoming SAP Partner webinar, we’re unpacking SAP Business Data Cloud from a solution architect’s perspective.  Not theory.  Real architecture conversations.  Real design considerations.  Real opportunities for partners.

We’ll cover how SAP BDC brings together SAP and non-SAP data, structured and unstructured sources, governance, analytics, and AI into one connected approach.

You’ll also hear about reusable data products, object store persistence, SAP Databricks integration, and best practices partners can apply with customers immediately.

And yes, there’s a demo too.  Because architecture diagrams are helpful but seeing it work changes the conversation.

If your customers are exploring AI, analytics modernization, or data harmonization, this session is worth your time.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jul/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010852.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BusinessAI #DataProducts #SolutionArchitecture #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified – SAP Business Data Cloud Fast-Track Session
entry.content:
SAP Business Data Cloud is moving fast.  Customers are asking bigger questions about data, AI, governance, and modernization.  Partners need answers that connect strategy with execution.

This Partner Learning Accelerator helps you prepare for the SAP Certified – SAP Business Data Cloud exam in a focused 3-hour live session built for experienced SAP Partners.

What you’ll explore:
• SAP BDC architecture and governance
• SAP BW modernization approaches
• SAP Databricks and AI use cases
• Delta sharing scenarios
• Storyflow execution from the SAP BDC cockpit
• Practical certification tips from certified experts

Plus, you’ll hear insights that go beyond the exam content and connect directly to customer conversations.

If SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere are already part of your toolkit, you’re in the right place.

Seats fill quickly.  Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010285.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPDatabricks #AI #SAPBusinessDataCloud #DigitalTransformation

entry.title: Planning your transition paths to SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Every architecture conversation with customers ends up at the same point: how do we move without disruption, and still create room for new ideas?

This SAP Partners session focuses on transition paths to SAP Business Data Cloud, and the options you can bring into those discussions right away.

We will explore greenfield and migration approaches, and what each means in real customer situations.  You will also see how SAP HANA Cloud and SAP BDC connect through bi-directional data exchange, helping hybrid landscapes stay connected instead of split.

Plus, we will look at how SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Databricks fit into the same picture, so data products feel connected across the stack.

Honestly, the thing is, most projects do not follow a single clean path.  This session helps you explain those mixed realities in a simple way.

If you are shaping data architecture with customers, this is worth joining.

Register now and join the conversation.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jul/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010869.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPBDC #DataStrategy

entry.title: Embracing AI for planning and analysis in SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
AI is moving fast.  Partners are asking sharper questions, customers expect smarter answers, and the pressure to turn data into action keeps growing.

That’s exactly why I’m excited about this upcoming session: “Embracing AI for planning and analysis in SAP Business Data Cloud.”

We’ll explore how AI is shaping analytics and planning across SAP Business Data Cloud, including innovations like Joule, Just Ask, and AI-Assisted capabilities available today, plus a look into the future roadmap.

The thing is, AI conversations can sometimes feel abstract.  This session keeps it practical.  Think faster insights, less manual effort, and better planning decisions across finance and operations.

Plus, we’ll leave time for live Q&A, because the best conversations usually happen when partners bring real-world scenarios to the table.

If your customers are asking how AI fits into planning and analytics, this webinar will give you useful talking points and examples you can apply right away.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jul/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010880.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPBusinessDataCloud #BusinessAI #Analytics #Planning

entry.title: Why SAP Document AI Matters More Than Most Enterprises Think
entry.content:
Introduction

When everyone gets excited about the same topics, conversations will naturally center around them. Eventually, that excitement will catch on, and you’ll find yourself on the same wavelength as everyone else. Currently, as businesses focus on enterprise AI projects, most discussions revolve around similar themes: copilot, agents, and large language models. However, despite significant investments in AI, many businesses still face a common operational obstacle: documents.

Regardless of the industry, invoices, purchase orders, supplier forms, shipping documents, contracts, and compliance documents flow between departments daily. Each requires some degree of manual verification or validation and often involves multiple systems or workflows. This is where SAP Document AI comes in.

SAP Document AI transforms unstructured business documents into structured operational data, helping businesses modernize their operations. Instead of just digitizing your documents, you can use Document AI to make them useful and functional within your business. Even with advancements in intelligent front-end experiences, the foundational infrastructure supporting these solutions still relies on manual verification, various data extraction methods, and disconnected workflows. Document intelligence within SAP environments has matured, and companies now view it as a key automation initiative rather than a secondary option.

Our Experience

Adopting Document AI can reduce the need for labor to support business functions, leading to more efficient solutions. SAP Document AI addresses a common challenge in enterprise environments: extracting, understanding, and organizing information from business documents. In many companies, important operational data arrives in formats like PDFs, scanned files, emails, invoices, and forms, which ERP systems struggle to process without manual help.

SAP Document AI lowers this friction by using AI to identify document types, extract relevant fields, classify information, and prepare that data for business workflows. This means organizations can automate many document-heavy processes across finance, procurement, supply chain, HR, and other operational areas, rather than having employees manually review documents and transfer information between systems. The value of using Document AI goes beyond faster document processing; it empowers operational workflows to operate with greater speed, consistency, and scalability. As enterprises manage more operational data, minimizing manual document reliance becomes essential for maintaining efficiency.

We had the opportunity to see this first-hand. In this scenario, the company was operating in the oil and gas industry, where customer representatives and field engineers constantly generate operational documents while working across remote locations. Every service ticket, carrier document, export filing, client approval, invoice, or credit memo needed to be attached to the correct Sales Order inside SAP for compliance and operational traceability purposes. The challenge was that these documents arrived through emails in different languages, layouts, and formats, often with little context beyond a subject line. As a result, employees had to manually open every file, identify what type of document it was, locate the Sales Order reference somewhere inside the content, rename the file according to internal naming conventions, and upload it into SAP. At a scale of more than 8,000 documents per month, this translated into over 1,000 hours of manual effort every month, distributed across teams in multiple regions.

The organization already had partial automation in place through legacy capture tools and batch jobs, but the process still depended heavily on human intervention before the automation could even begin. A single typo in a file name or an incorrectly classified document could silently break the workflow and prevent the document from being linked correctly inside SAP. By implementing SAP Document AI on SAP BTP, the process shifted from manual interpretation to intelligent document processing. Incoming documents were automatically picked up through SAP Integration Suite, classified using SAP Document AI, and processed to extract key operational data such as Sales Order numbers, vendor details, amounts, and document dates. The extracted information was then validated and routed directly into SAP workflows, significantly reducing manual effort while improving scalability, consistency, and operational visibility across the entire process.

Why This Matters Beyond Document Processing

What made this project valuable was not just the automation itself. I would say it was rather the impact it had on daily operations. Once documents could be processed and validated in a more consistent way, teams spent far less time trying to interpret files manually or fixing small mistakes that slowed everything down. Workflows became easier to manage across different regions, and the process no longer depended so heavily on people knowing specific naming conventions or internal steps that were never formally documented.

Over time, we also realized that SAP Document AI was doing much more than helping with document processing. It became part of the operational structure supporting the entire workflow. As companies continue investing in SAP Business AI and intelligent workflows, having reliable operational data becomes increasingly important. If documents are still disconnected from the systems around them, scaling automation becomes much harder than expected.

Conclusion

As we said at the beginning, a lot of the conversation around enterprise AI focuses on what people can immediately see: copilots, AI agents, chat interfaces, and new user experiences. At the same time, many operational problems still happen behind the scenes, especially in processes where important business information is buried inside emails, PDFs, scanned files, and manual workflows.

Our experience with SAP Document AI showed us that document intelligence is no longer just a secondary automation tool. It can play an important role in helping businesses create cleaner and more scalable operations. When documents become structured operational data instead of isolated files, workflows move faster, teams spend less time on repetitive tasks, and enterprise systems become easier to scale over time.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, May 21st, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

2026 Developer Insights Survey Results

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/the-2026-developer-insights-survey-the-report/ba-p/14391535

UX Introducing the new SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

  • Library Site: https://fal.cloud.sap/
  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-the-new-sap-fiori-apps-reference-library/ba-p/14346676

Live Report from Sapphire, Spain

UX Innovation Day in Silicon Valley – June 11th

  • Event Site: hhttps://events.sap.com/us-ux-innovation-day-siliconvalley-2026/en_us/home.html

DYK 17 – Read Only Preview Feature in ABAP Cleaner

  • ABAP Cleaner Project: https://github.com/SAP/abap-cleaner

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 2026 Developer Insights Survey Results

0:54 Introducing the New SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

2:04 Live Report from Sapphire, Spain

4:25 UX Innovation Day in Silicon valley on June 11th

5:23 DKY #17 Read Only Preview feature in ABAP Cleaner

Transcription

00;00;00;04 - 00;00;06;11
Intro
This is the SAP Developer News for May the 21st, 2026.

00;00;06;14 - 00;00;09;26
Mamikee Kanneh
results of the 2026 SAP Developer Insight Survey are

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;31;10
Mamikee Kanneh
now live, and there are some really interesting trends in this year's report. We're seeing continued growth in AI adoption with developers not only using AI tools in production, but also using them to learn and explore new concepts. We also saw a continued rise in solution and application architects across the community, showing how developer roles are evolving beyond just coding.

00;00;31;15 - 00;00;54;06
Mamikee Kanneh
Now for learning enablement. The report highlights how developers are learning today and the resources they rely on the most. The report also looks at trends across cloud development, developer tooling, languages, frameworks, and the broader SAP developer ecosystem. Now, if you're interested in the results and won't explore the findings in more detail, check out the full report in the link below.

00;00;54;08 - 00;01;23;04
Thomas Jung
In this developer news segment, we want to tell you about a new tool that SAP has recently made available. The Fiori Apps Reference Library. Now this is actually something that from the name you might not know what all it does. It actually breaks down all the different SAP provided pieces of functionality, business functionality like different transaction codes or different applications, and tells you what UI technology that they currently use.

00;01;23;12 - 00;01;51;12
Thomas Jung
This is a question that I've gotten a lot over the years from people wanting to know, well, how can I see what all Fiori UIs are available? But this also shows you the older technologies as well. It lists SAPGUI applications, Web Dynpro, other types. So it can really help you in your process as you're trying to catalog or move to move to the cloud or modernize, particularly your S/4 environment.

00;01;51;12 - 00;02;06;19
Thomas Jung
So this is a great new tool for developers and business experts alike. Be sure to check it out. The link to the tool will be in the show notes as always.

00;02;06;22 - 00;02;20;07
Antonio Maradiaga
We are at the show floor here in Sapphire Madrid. As you can see in the back. We are at the agent lab where it's been quite busy this past couple of days. Lots of interest around Joule Studio. What can you tell us about it? Kevin?

00;02;20;08 - 00;02;40;14
Kevin Riedelsheimer
Antonio, I got to say, everybody's really excited to get their hands on the fresh and brand new Joule Studio. They're very excited about the capabilities of SAP, providing 50 years of business acumen right there in Joule Studio. So they have everything at their fingertips, not only AI assisted coding, but also all of SAP's knowledge right there in the tool.

00;02;40;14 - 00;02;42;02
Kevin Riedelsheimer
So they love that.

00;02;42;04 - 00;02;44;17
Antonio Maradiaga
What about you, Witalij? What have you seen?

00;02;44;19 - 00;03;04;18
Witalij Rudnicki
I don't think that three days here at Sapphire turned me into a marketing person, just like some other colleagues of mine became. I still see this as a technology. It's exciting technology. It's really interesting to learn. There are lots of questions, not only technical questions, but, well, philosophical questions about the future of work, about the future of development.

00;03;04;18 - 00;03;13;23
Witalij Rudnicki
And I must say that during these three days, I had more philosophical discussions than during my previous 25 years in SAP ecosystem.

00;03;13;25 - 00;03;15;27
Antonio Maradiaga
Excellent and Hola.

00;03;16;00 - 00;03;19;13
Josh (Bluebeard) Bentley
SAP community.

00;03;19;16 - 00;03;41;05
Josh (Bluebeard) Bentley
I'm so happy to be back with the developer advocates. We've had an amazing few days here in Madrid. We were at a Stomtisch meetup last night. All of us got there, enjoyed conversation with David and the local team as well as others who came up from Barcelona. Here it has been full the entire show. People coding with Joule Studio using this technology in ways that they've been learning about in the ecosystem, and now seeing it in action in a preview model.

00;03;41;05 - 00;03;58;10
Josh (Bluebeard) Bentley
This is a staging system. It's not fully productive yet. We're hoping to have a sign up for a playground system so people can actually use this in the community as soon as possible. August timeframe right before Q3 when it goes GA. So Joule Studio super popular, a complete reinvent of how SAP is doing the modeling and the building of agents.

00;03;58;10 - 00;04;10;16
Josh (Bluebeard) Bentley
And it's just a great time to be talking about all the stuff happening in this world that we work with. So much cool technology, and I think a great time to talk about even more will be at TechEd. So I'll see you at TechEd in late October in Berlin.

00;04;10;22 - 00;04;26;10
Witalij Rudnicki
And next time you are in Madrid and you are attending Amitabh here. Remember, there are more than one tap rooms here, so check, double check what address you need to be going to say.

00;04;26;12 - 00;04;54;23
Thomas Jung
A few weeks ago, we told you about a upcoming UX Innovation Day in the Silicon Valley area that will be taking place on June 11th, and we thought this would be a good opportunity to remind you of that, because there's still time to register and plan to attend. And coming out of Sapphire last week, it's pretty obvious to everybody that AI is going to have a huge impact on, well, everything in our ecosystem.

00;04;54;23 - 00;05;26;19
Thomas Jung
But the user experience is a big part of that impact. So this session that's being planned on UX innovation will focus on the impact of AI. So if you've got lots of questions or ideas coming out of all the analysis from Sapphire, be sure to attend this session in Silicon Valley. The link to register or find out more details is in the show notes as always.

00;05;26;22 - 00;05;54;06
Sheena M K
Did you know that you can preview your cleaned code using ABAP Cleaner without locking or changing the code? Let me show you how in seconds. First, select any amount of ABAP code in the current code editor in ADT. Next press Ctrl+Shift+five or select Menu ->Source Code->Show->Read Only Preview with ABAP Cleaner. This opens the interactive ABAP Cleaner UI, but you can notice that the apply button is not available.

00;05;54;13 - 00;06;03;15
Sheena M K
Here you can not only get a preview of your code with the suggestions from ABAP Cleaner, but also copy the code from the clipboard or file without making any

entry.title: Is SAP Still a Software Company? Reflections from Sapphire 2026
entry.content:
Like many of you, we attended SAP Sapphire Orlando 2026 last week. Before it, our minds were already set on the most likely topics the conversations would revolve around. We thought about the usual when it comes to enterprise AI themes: copilots, new generative capabilities, maybe the launching of something unexpected. Of course, those topics were covered, and we were right to assume that they would be the center of the event. However, the keynote didn’t waste any time and soon revealed that we were there for something much bigger; we were there to witness the first steps towards a bigger strategic shift that is taking place inside SAP. The discussion was no longer just about adding AI features into enterprise software, but about redefining the role ERP systems could play in the next generation of enterprise AI execution.

Over the past two years, most organizations have been implementing copilots that help employees be more productive. For instance, employees can send emails faster, write meeting minutes faster, and look for information easier. However, once enterprises attempt to implement AI on a larger scale, it becomes clear how difficult it is for businesses to actually make things work. Recommendations can be made but putting them into practice in an environment full of disparate systems, workflows, approvals, and data sources can be extremely hard.

This is precisely why SAP's approach to AI seems so unique. Instead of presenting AI as another layer separate from the core of enterprise operations, SAP is attempting to integrate AI into the very systems where business processes take place. As one of the speakers emphasized during their keynote presentation, ERP is "the brain of every company." This idea formed the basis for all the presentations at Sapphire. SAP's view on enterprise AI is that it becomes much more valuable when it operates with process awareness, governance, semantic business context, and operational data.

One of the key announcements made at Sapphire is SAP Business AI Platform. Surprisingly, SAP did not introduce it as "another AI offering". This product is positioned as an architectural layer connecting enterprise AI to operational systems and business processes. The purpose here is to merge ERP process knowledge, Business Data Cloud, AI orchestration, governance frameworks, and semantic business context into a single enterprise AI layer for executing actions, not just giving suggestions.

Another dominant topic at the Sapphire event was moving from AI assistants to operational AI agents. SAP introduced hundreds of AI agents and assistants for Finance, Procurement, Supply Chain, HCM, and Customer Experience processes. However, the number of them was not really the point of their announcement. Much greater attention was paid to orchestration. SAP clearly communicated that enterprise AI will not succeed with isolated copilots, but rather with integrated AI systems that can interact in operational processes. In other words, the real challenge today is not just implementing AI solutions. The goal is to achieve operationalizing them within the enterprise.

Especially fascinating about this approach is its implications for enterprise architecture going forward. Traditionally, enterprises have always treated enterprise software as an assembly of individual platforms that can be integrated. But now, AI is starting to reveal the limitations of such an approach. The more fragmented the operational environment becomes, the less effective it is for AI algorithms to work. This is likely the reason behind SAP’s long focus on topics such as Business Data Cloud, semantic layer, governance models, and process orchestration. It looks like SAP believes that the future success of enterprise AI lies not in how well the individual models perform but rather in the level of their connectivity and operational awareness.

This also signals an important shift for SAP partners and customers. Until recently, discussions around digital transformation revolved around implementation, migration, and modernization. But as enterprise AI becomes more prominent, the focus is shifting to operational readiness. Clean data structures, process standardization, governance models, and ERP systems become prerequisites for successful AI adoption rather than just IT requirements. In a way, Sapphire 2026 suggests that the most capable adopters of AI are not necessarily those experimenting with the greatest number of copilots, but those with the most robust operational foundations to support them.

Following Sapphire 2026, it becomes increasingly clear that SAP tries to evolve beyond being defined as a software company. It seeks to position itself as the operational layer for AI, merging ERP logic, enterprise data, governance, and AI orchestration into a single architecture. If this vision is going to materialize is yet to be seen, but the direction is hard to deny. SAP believes that the next wave of competitive advantages in enterprise AI does not lie in the strength of the AI models but in the ownership of the operational systems that execute AI-driven business decisions.

entry.title: Get Early Insights to SAP AI Innovations: Join SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 by June 19
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire, SAP reaffirmed its strong commitment to AI-driven innovation and its role in transforming businesses.

Innovation at SAP is built on collaboration. Leveraging cutting-edge technologies like Artificial Intelligence, SAP continues to drive meaningful business value for customers. Now, we invite you to be part of this journey by participating in SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects focused on AI.

🗓Registration is open until June 19, 2026.

In today’s fast-evolving business landscape, early involvement is key. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can collaborate directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing feedback on concepts, prototypes, and innovations while they are still taking shape.

View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the selected exciting opportunities:

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-Assisted Error Remediation for SAP Application Interface Framework
  • AI assisted guidance in the UI theme designer
  • BTP Specific Data Privacy & AI based Data Masking
  • Fulfillment of business needs through Business Adaption Plans and Solution Implementation
  • Partner Perspectives in SAP BTP and AI development

Cloud ERP

  • Accelerate Integrations with AI powered SAP Integration Suite
  • Agentic AI in SAP S/4HANA Procurement (Private Cloud Edition)
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
  • AI-Powered Warranty Verification – Co-Innovate the future of service, repair & claims
  • Intelligent Returns: Process Discovery & Automation Roadmap
  • Modernization Approaches in Purchase Order (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)

SAP Globalization

  • AI for UI: Exploring the Usage of AI in Translating SAP's Product User Interface
  • Assessing Demand for AI Speech Services in SAP Translation Hub

SAP Concur

  • Concur Invoice - AI-first, Reimagine Invoice Experience

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative meets SAP Integration Experience Feedback

  • Hands-on: AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Mgmt - Provisioning & Setup
  • Hands-on Experience of AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Management
  • Hands-on System Experience of the AI-Assisted Process Modeler in SAP Signavio

Others

  • Autonomous Supply Chain: Embodied AI in the Warehouse
  • Embodied AI Autonomous Asset Inspection
  • Embodied AI: Autonomous Visual Inspection for Quality and Safety
  • Iterate together on the User Experience for Joule Work
  • Providing feedback on the Discovery domain in SAP LeanIX

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Integrating Joule with SAP SuccessFactor
entry.content:
As organizations accelerate their digital HR transformation, the demand for intelligent, intuitive, and employee-centric solutions has never been higher. SAP Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot, brings a new layer of intelligence to SAP SuccessFactors by enabling natural language interactions, real-time insights, and guided decision-making across HR processes. Together, Joule and SuccessFactors redefine how HR teams and employees engage with talent data—shifting from transactional workflows to proactive, insight-driven experiences.

When integrated with SAP SuccessFactors, Joule acts as a smart HR assistant embedded directly into HR workflows. SAP Joule with SAP SuccessFactors transforms the way organizations manage and experience HR by combining intelligent automation with contextual, AI-driven insights. From enabling natural language queries and guided HR actions to delivering real-time workforce intelligence and seamless employee self-service, Joule simplifies complex HR processes across the organization. These capabilities not only empower HR teams and leaders to make faster, data-backed decisions but also reduce administrative workload, accelerate access to insights for managers, and enhance the overall employee experience through conversational HR. As a result, organizations achieve stronger adoption of SAP SuccessFactors features while unlocking greater strategic value from their HR technology investment.

Here, we outline the key steps involved in integrating SAP Joule with SAP SuccessFactors to enable intelligent, AI-driven HR experiences across your organization.

Pre-requisites:

  • SAP BTP Subaccount with Joule(das-application) Check the entitlements in your SAP BTP Global Account.
  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition / SAP Start.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services which usually will be delivered with SF tenant.

Roles required to configure:

  • SAP BTP Global Account Admin & BTP Subaccount Admin (in case they are different users)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Administrator
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services Administrator

Procedure:

  • Activating Joule and Running the Booster
  • Establish Trust with your Cloud Identity, BTP and Create Trusted Domains.
  • Activating Joule in SF.
  • Activate Cloud Foundry, Create Destinations and Work zone Content Provider.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Source & Target Configurations

License:

Joule is included as part of your SAP SuccessFactors license at no additional cost with a certain number of free messages for an annual period known as Base AI. Some of the AI Capabilities are part of the premium edition - Premium AI, where you may have to purchase AI Units to use the functionalities.

  • SuccessFactors customers will receive a Joule message allocation based on the number of licensed, active users; Users licensed for multiple products only count once.
  • Messages will be metered on an annual basis; at the end of each term, the annual allocation of free messages will reset (i.e., no carryover)
  • Customers must purchase additional SAP AI Units (Premium AI) if they exceed their free message allocation during the annual period
  • In case the service is not visible in your BTP Cockpit; to gain access to Joule, all customers will need to license a no-cost SAP SKU. Doing so will trigger the provisioning of Joule and the creation of the terms and conditions that must be accepted by the customer to use Joule.

Architecture Diagram:

 

Configuration Steps:

  • In BTP cockpit check if the Sucessfactor System is connected to BTP System Landscape and registered. If the system is not connected, we need to manually add the system to the landscape by registering the token successfully with SF.
  • Create a New subaccount in BTP cockpit for SF Joule.
  • Establish trust between the IAS and the BTP cockpit in the subaccount trusted configuration by adding the IAS URL.
  • Add the SF URL to the trusted domain in both BTP and IAS.
  • Create SAP Build Work zone service in the SF subaccount.
  • Run the Joule Booster.

 

Select the respective IAS, Build workzone and the SF tenant and finish the Booster run.

 

  • Login to SF with admin user and create a group, add the enable joule role to this group and assign to the users for enabling Joule icon. This group is required to be added to the users who are accessing the Joule to get the Joule Icon for them.

 

  • Enable cloud foundry and create a service key
  • A new role “Launchpad_Admin” will be created in BTP cockpit after creating the service key. Assign it to the configuration user before performing next steps.
  • The next step is to create destinations. Destinations LPS_SFSF_rt and SFSF_SSO are already created when running the booster.
  • Create a new destination named Navigation service. Open the service key and copy the portal service, client id, client secret and URL and paste in the destinations.
  • Create second destination named LPS_SFSFS_dt. For URL please follow the note 2215682.
  • In Build work zone URL and create a new content provider and give the design time and runtime destinations and check the report which will give the assigned roles imported to the content provider.

 

 

  • Login to IAS and check the sf.api.version for SF source system is 2. If it is 1 then we need to get the Json files from SAP portal for source and target systems and import it. For version 2 no action needed

 

Login to IAS and Import the downloaded Source and target file.

Then Download the certificate from IAS in source system and Import in SF.

 

 

  • Similarly Import the target file to IAS.
  • Finally run the read job in IAS to sync the configuration. We can see the logs from provisioning logs once it is completed. After the job is completed, we can see the roles assigned to specific users in work zone

Conclusion:

This completes the configuration of Joule for SF. Users can ask for SF queries to Joule and Joule will respond back. Please go through the blog and provide your valuable feedback and feel free to reach out to me in comment section for any doubts.

entry.title: From Form to Tile: Add an SAP BPA start form in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Introduction:

This guide shows how to surface an SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BPA) start form as a tile in SAP Build Work Zone, so users can trigger workflows directly from a Work Zone site. The steps apply in general, whether or not your tenant is integrated with SAP IPD Collaboration

You’ll learn how to collect the required IDs, configure the app parameters and navigation, adjust the tile’s visualization and translations, and grant role access. By the end, your site will offer a reliable, one-click entry point to launch your SAP BPA workflow.

This article provides a detailed, screenshot-rich walkthrough of the official Help documentation: see the Help page.

 

Prerequisites:

To follow this guide, you need a deployed workflow in SAP BPA with a form as the trigger and a Work Zone tenant with SAP IPD Collaboration enabled.

For more information about creating a workflow and setting up SAP Build Work Zone, please refer to the following blogs:

  • Developing Processes in SAP Build Process Automation for Integrated Product Development by @Alexandra_Rong
  • SAP Build Work Zone in Integrated Product Development: A Step-by-Step Setup Guide by @Aylinborzan

 

Step 1: Get the required information

To create a tile on your preferred Work Zone site, you need details from the SAP BPA start form. The required IDs are available as part of a link in the workflow where the form is configured as the trigger. Make sure you are viewing the deployed version of the workflow. The IDs do not change with each release cycle; they only change if you modify the trigger. Therefore, if you adjust the workflow or update fields in the form (without changing the trigger), you do not need to update the IDs in the Work Zone content settings.

In the deployed version of the workflow, click the trigger that uses the form. A details panel opens on the right. There you’ll find two links: one opens the form in a new window so you can start the workflow, and the other provides configuration details. The configuration link is required to connect to SAP Build Work Zone. Copy the link and paste it into a notes app.

 

Step 2: Check if “Process Trigger“ is already visible in “Content Manager“

To check if the “Process Trigger“ app is already visible in “Content Manager“, navigate to the “Content Manager“ in the SAP Build Work Zone.

There are multiple apps with the name “Process Trigger“, so make sure that it is from the channel “HTML5 Apps“. If you can see it in “Content Manager,” continue with Step 3.

Otherwise click on “Content Explorer“ on the upper right side next to the create button. There click on “HTML5 Apps“ with the subtitle “saas_approuter“.

Then select the app “Process Trigger“ and click on “Add“ in the upper right corner.

Afterwards, go back to “Content Manager“ and verify that “Process Trigger” now appears in the list.

 

Step 3: Create the app in SAP Build Work Zone

To create the app in SAP Build Work Zone, go to “Content Manager“ via the left navigation.

There you can see all sites, apps, and roles you have already created. Open the app “Process Trigger“ from the channel “HTML5 Apps“ by clicking the row.

This app is a template for the tile you want to create. You will see information such as your subaccount ID, which will be reused for your tile. Click the “Create a Local Copy” button in the upper-right corner to copy the template.

A new app based on the template opens. Click “Edit” in the upper-right corner to add all required information for your start form.

First, change the “Application Title“ and enter a description. The application title will be used as the tile title later.

Then go to the “Navigation” tab. There you find intent and parameters as section. Change the “Semantic Object“ and “Action“ to a fitting name for your form and enter the default value for the “uri“ parameter. The default value is the full URL you copied from SAP BPA, including all IDs.

Next, adjust the tile visualization in the “Visualization” tab. You can change the subtitle, add additional information, and update the displayed icon. The tile title matches the application title you set earlier.

In the “Translation“ tab, you can add translations for your tile. Make sure to update the translated text for English at minimum, since the translation is copied from the template.

In the “Additional Info” tab, you can see who created the app and when it was last changed. No action is required here.

Now save your changes by clicking on the button ”Save” in the upper right corner. 

Your tile has been successfully created.

 

Step 4: Add the tile to the site

After creating the tile for your start form, add it to your site which you can select in the ”Content Manager”.

Open the site where you want the tile to appear and click in the upper-right corner on ”Edit”.

Then you can add the tile of your newly created form in your preferred section by clicking on the ”+” in the upper-right corner of the section.

In the pop-up, select your tile and add it. After adding the tile, you can change the position via drag and drop. Then save your changes on the site by clicking the ”Save” button in the upper-right corner.

 

Step 5: Grant roles access to the tile

You’ve created a tile for your start form and added it to your preferred site in SAP Build Work Zone. Next, grant role access so users can see the tile. Without this, the tile won’t be visible to anyone.

In ”Content Manager”, open the user role you want to grant access to.

Then click on the upper-right side on ”Edit” and activate the ”Assignment Status” for your tile. Save your changes by clicking on the ”Save” button.

Conclusion

You have now set up an SAP BPA start form as a tile in SAP Build Work Zone. By collecting the required IDs from your workflow, configuring the app parameters and navigation, and assigning the appropriate role access, your users can trigger SAP BPA workflows directly from their Work Zone site with a single click.
This setup works independently of SAP IPD Collaboration, making it a flexible approach for any Work Zone tenant.
If you run into issues or have questions, feel free to leave a comment below.

entry.title: Partner learning accelerator: SAP certified - Security administrator
entry.content:
Security and user access management are often behind the scenes.  Until something breaks.  Then everyone notices.

That’s why strong SAP security expertise matters more than ever across SAP S/4HANA and cloud environments.

Our upcoming “Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP Certified – Security Administrator” is designed to help SAP Partners prepare for the C_SEC certification through a focused 6-hour live session.  We’ll cover the topics partners deal with every day: authorization concepts, identity and access management, business role design, SAP Fiori authorizations, plus troubleshooting access issues across SAP systems.

The thing is, certification prep can feel overwhelming when you’re balancing customer projects and deadlines.  This session helps cut through the noise and focus on the exam domains that matter most.

If you already work with SAP system administration or user management, this is a great next step to sharpen your expertise and validate your skills.

Register now and join us.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/jun/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00010506.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPSecurity #SAPS4HANA #IdentityAccessManagement #SAPLearning

entry.title: Mastering SAP Business Data Cloud - Discovery workshop 2026-Q2
entry.content:
Certified in SAP Business Data Cloud?  Now learn how to bring it to life with customers.

The “Mastering SAP Business Data Cloud – Discovery Workshop 2026-Q2” is designed for SAP Partner sales and presales teams ready to sharpen discovery conversations and lead with business value.

Two days.  Real workshop simulations.  Customer scenarios.  Architecture discussions. Group presentations.  Live coaching.

No long theory sessions.  No passive listening.

You’ll practice how to:
• Lead value advisory conversations
• Position SAP Business Data Cloud with confidence
• Guide customer discovery workshops
• Connect business challenges to solution outcomes

Plus, the multiplier model helps you scale these skills across your teams and customer engagements.

If you want your next customer meeting to feel more strategic, more engaging, and far less transactional, this workshop is for you.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014170.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPBusinessDataCloud #Presales #DataDriven #PartnerSuccess

entry.title: Start Small. Think at Scale. Business Professionals Can Use SAP & Claude to Build a Business Case.
entry.content:
Note: This blog provides an actionable step-by-step proof of concept (POC) for the SAP and Anthropic partnership that a business professional can build today using SAP's free Business Accelerator Hub sandbox data and Claude. The Master Prompt is included below. A sample output is provided in the Addendum. Already familiar with this context? Skip directly to The Proof of Concept section.

Build the POC. Create the Business Case. Start the Conversation.

The recent SAP and Anthropic partnership announcement ignited a new wave of opportunities. It got me thinking about how the combined capabilities of Claude and SAP will strengthen enterprises around the globe.


At the same time, I found myself wondering. How can Business Professionals add real value to what needs to happen to put these capabilities in place for maximum success?

It reminded me of a simple metaphor. Imagine digging a new swimming pool with a shovel. You are making progress. Slowly. Painfully. Then someone pulls up with a bulldozer. The hole that would have taken weeks gets done in a day. That is the uplift the SAP and Anthropic partnership represents for organizations who are ready to act.

So how can Business Professionals help jumpstart the process?

Consider the impact of creating a simple proof of concept using SAP's own sandbox data and Claude, grounded in business case best practices, to help prioritize initiatives that can create compelling value.

The Market Opportunity

The scale of this partnership is significant. SAP and Anthropic are not simply connecting two products. They are building an Autonomous Enterprise vision where Claude powered agents operate through SAP Business AI Platform, coordinate across SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Ariba, and other SAP products via Model Context Protocol (MCP), and are accessible to users through Joule.

IDC projects AI spending driven by agentic AI will reach $1.3 trillion by 2029, growing at 31.9% year over year. In a best case scenario Gartner projects a $450 billion enterprise AI opportunity by 2035. Yet according to the DSAG Investment Report published in February 2026, among SAP customers already using AI, only 3% are running SAP Business AI in production today.

The opportunity is enormous. But before Business Professionals can seize it, they must consider creating POCs and business cases that will move their organization from insight to action.

The New API Policy. A Quality Framework.

It is worth highlighting a recent SAP announcement that is directly relevant to this partnership. SAP's API policy governs how autonomous external agents make sequential API calls into SAP systems.

Consider this approach as a quality framework.

Think Lean Six Sigma, ITIL, or ISO 9001 for mission critical Cloud ERP processes. Controlled variation. Trusted inputs. Governed outputs.

Claude operating inside SAP's approved architecture brings these same quality principles to agentic AI. As a Six Sigma certified practitioner I can say with confidence that controlled variation and governed outputs are not optional in mission critical processes. They are the standard.

Errors in enterprise data cannot be fully eliminated but they must be managed, minimized, and controlled continuously. They stem from uncontrolled inputs, ungoverned processes, and systems operating outside defined boundaries.

SAP's framework controls that variation at the source, reducing risk to the lowest achievable level and keeping mission critical processes operating with the precision enterprises depend on.

From Insight to Action. Building Your Case

Consider this scenario. You are a Business Professional who has just learned about the SAP and Anthropic partnership. You see the opportunity clearly. You know your organization has processes that AI can benefit, especially with Claude and SAP working together.

But where do you start?

You are not a developer. You do not have a project budget approved. You cannot stand up a Joule environment this afternoon.

What you do have is business knowledge, SAP familiarity, and a growing confidence in what Claude can do. Most importantly you have a clear sense of where the pain and inefficiencies are in your organization today.

That is enough to get started.

The path forward has two parallel tracks that reinforce each other:

  1. Delivering a proof of concept (POC) that shows what is possible
  2. Creating a business case that justifies the investment to make it real at scale.

The good news is that SAP has already given you the tools to do both.

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub was built explicitly to accelerate customer time to value. Free to access. No SAP license required. No IT ticket needed. Just a free SAP ID and a clear business question you want Claude to help answer.

The proof of concept you build today becomes the evidence that funds the enterprise deployment tomorrow. The business case you build around it gives your organization's executive sponsors the confidence to act.

This approach is how Business Professionals provide meaningful leadership, collaborating with IT counterparts and key stakeholders to move from insight to action.

The Proof of Concept  

This POC is intentionally simple and uses SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition procurement data available through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox. It is a minimum viable product (MVP) approach designed to show what is possible without requiring engineering support, IT involvement, or a live SAP system. Every step is something a Business Professional can execute independently.

What you are about to build is a working example of agentic AI. Claude is not simply answering a question. It is reasoning over real SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition data, identifying risk, prioritizing action, and recommending next steps. That is agentic AI in action. And it is a preview of what runs inside Joule at enterprise scale.

The following steps walk you through the POC from start to finish.

What You Do

  1. Register for a free SAP ID at api.sap.com. Takes a few minutes.
  2. Go to the Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. This is the current OData V4 Purchase Order API for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and one example of the many sandbox APIs available across SAP's core product portfolio.
  3. Click “Try Out” at the top. Select “Sandbox” from the dropdown on the right side. Navigate to the Purchase Order section in the left hand navigation. Click the /PurchaseOrder “GET” option. Click “Run.”
  4. Copy the JSON response that appears under “Response Body.”
  5. Open Claude at claude.ai. Paste the data and enter the Master Prompt below. Note: A free Claude account is sufficient for this POC. Claude Pro provides higher usage limits if needed.

What Claude Does

  1. Reads and interprets the SAP S/4HANA Cloud purchase order data.
  2. Identifies which exceptions represent the highest business risk.
  3. Assigns a risk score to each record with a clear rationale.
  4. Recommends specific actions for each prioritized item.
  5. Produces a scored table ready for your business case conversation.
  6. Offers to draft a supplier communication for the highest risk item.

Master Prompt

Copy and paste the following prompt into Claude along with your JSON data (as noted above). You are reviewing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition purchase order data retrieved directly from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox via the Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001).

[START PROMPT]

Review the records provided and analyze the following fields: PurchaseOrder number, Supplier, CreationDate, PurchaseOrderDate, PurchasingProcessingStatus, PurchaseOrderDeletionCode, ReleaseIsNotCompleted, and PurchasingReleaseStrategy.

Based on your analysis:

  1. Identify purchase orders that require attention. Focus on deletion flagged records, incomplete releases, unusual processing statuses, and supplier concentration patterns.
  2. Assign a priority level of High, Medium, or Low to each flagged record with a brief rationale.
  3. Recommend a specific action for each flagged item.
  4. Produce a prioritized table with the following columns: PO Number, Supplier, Creation Date, Processing Status, Deletion Flag, Release Complete, Priority Level, Recommended Action.
  5. Provide a two to three sentence plain English summary of your overall findings before the table.
  6. After the table offer to draft a supplier communication for the highest priority item.
  7. Keep your total response concise. Summary, table, and offer only. No additional commentary unless asked.

[END PROMPT]

What You Just Built

A working example of agentic AI reasoning over real SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition procurement data. Built by a Business Professional. Using SAP's own tools. View the sample output in the Addendum below

This is your building block. The cornerstone of your business case. And the start of a meaningful conversation with your IT counterparts, key stakeholders, and executive sponsors about what is possible and the value this use case can deliver.

Start small. Think at scale.

From POC to Production

This POC uses the SAP Business Accelerator Hub sandbox as a starting point outside of SAP's production architecture. That is intentional. The goal is to demonstrate the concept quickly and accessibly. Free sandbox data. No infrastructure required.

Joule Studio is where developers and business teams can go next. A governed, enterprise grade environment where Business Professionals and developers collaborate to build both pre-built and custom agents tailored to their specific processes, data, and workflows. Joule Studio requires SAP BTP, a Joule subscription, SAP Build licensing, and IT provisioning to activate. It is the right next step. See Activate Joule Studio in 5 Simple Steps for full prerequisites.  

Complementary approaches. Each with its own role.

In production this same capability runs natively inside SAP Business AI Platform through the MCP Gateway. It is accessible to users through Joule. Everything you just demonstrated is the reasoning capability that sits behind the potential production implementation. The logic transfers directly.

In this POC the Hub provides everything you need to show what is possible.

Building the Business Case

A strong business case requires the right elements addressed with clarity and evidence. The POC you just built directly addresses two of the most critical. It defines the problem in concrete terms and demonstrates measurable value, ROI potential, and a clear view of what is possible.

The implementation path and risk and governance elements are addressed by SAP's approved architecture. The path runs through SAP Business AI Platform, Joule, and the production framework SAP and Anthropic have built together.

That represents a credible and governed foundation for any business case conversation.


Security and Data You Can Depend On

The same security and governance framework that protects your SAP environment today protects every action Claude takes inside it. The controls do not change because AI is involved. They extend to cover it.

Every action is governed by the same role-based access controls your organization operates under today. Every decision Claude makes is traceable through a full audit trail.

Connecting external data sources to the SAP environment requires proper integration through the SAP Integration Suite, ensuring data flows through governed, secure pathways before Claude ever touches it.

The Path Forward

The SAP and Anthropic partnership is designed for enterprise scale, grounded in trust and quality outcomes. Today, Business Professionals can create a tangible starting point and play a leadership role in the conversation with IT and business stakeholders about initiatives that can create lasting value.

SAP and Anthropic have announced plans to bring Claude to SAP Business AI Platform. Joule Studio, the development environment where agents are built and deployed, has general availability planned for later this year.

Now is the time to build your POC and business case so your organization is ready.

Take it one step at a time.

Start small. Think at scale.

Join the Conversation

Your likes and comments are welcome and appreciated.

  • Are you excited about the SAP and Anthropic partnership?
  • Were you aware of the SAP Business Accelerator Hub?
  • What are you working on with Claude or your favorite Assistant AI that can be applied to the Autonomous Enterprise vision?

About the Author

Rich Blumberg is an award-winning writer and author of two Amazon published books, Novi’s Journey to the Magic Garden and Job Seeking Warriors – A Mentors Guide to Winning. He's the President of World Sales Solutions, LLC, (WSS) a management consulting, low-code, no-code, and AI services firm. For more than 20 years he has worked as an SAP consultant partnering with SAP and their ecosystem organizations around the globe. Outside of work Rich serves as a Drexel University Alumni Board of Governors Emeritus, an ENGin English tutor supporting Ukrainian citizens, and an aspiring guitar player.

Related Blogs

  • How the AI Content Lifecycle Turns Complexity into Clarity at Scale(Part 2)
  • AI-Powered Knowledge Gap Analysis and Content Lifecycle Governance(Part 1)
  • You Can Build an Agentic AI Workflow. No Coding, No Engineering, No Problem.
  • AI at Warp Speed: Lessons from the Horse and Buggy Collapse

Addendum SAP S/4HANA Cloud POC Output

Purchase Order Analysis using the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox and Claude

Summary

Of the 50 purchase orders reviewed, 6 require immediate attention. Two records carry both a deletion flag and an incomplete release status, representing the highest risk. Four additional records show a deletion code with a completed processing status, suggesting they were cancelled but may warrant confirmation. Supplier 17300001 dominates the dataset with over 60% concentration, which itself warrants a supplier risk review.

Note: In a production environment this same analysis can run across thousands of purchase orders in seconds.

Prioritized Purchase Order Exception Table

Draft Supplier Communication

Highest Priority Item: Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022 | Supplier USSU-VSF01

Subject: Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022 – Urgent Status Clarification Required

Dear USSU-VSF01 Team,

I am writing regarding two purchase orders currently showing an unresolved status in our SAP S/4HANA system that require your immediate attention.

Purchase Orders 4500000021 and 4500000022, both dated August 18, 2016, are currently flagged for deletion while the approval release process remains incomplete. This combination of statuses prevents us from properly closing or reactivating these orders and creates a compliance risk in our procurement records.

We kindly request that you confirm the following by end of business this week:

  1. Whether any goods or services were delivered against either purchase order.
  2. Whether any outstanding invoices or payment obligations remain.
  3. Your agreement to formally close both orders if no outstanding obligations exist.

Please contact our procurement team at your earliest convenience so we can resolve this matter and ensure our records accurately reflect the current state of our relationship.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request.

Best regards,
Procurement Team
Company Code 1710

Data source: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Purchase Order API (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) via SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox. Analysis performed by

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2605
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2605

On May 16, the new Release 2605 was delivered. This blog post lists all delivered features and provides an overview. Highlights of the release include new ABAP Language features, the Manage News app for MyHome, the Review Booklets for embedded analytics, and the new Manage SAP System Upgrade app.

The list of all new features including more information can be found on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP environment page. The new AI features are listed separately on the What's New for ABAP Generative AI in ABAP Cloud. The first delivery of ADT for VS Code is independent from the ABAP Backend release and will be rolled-out separately. 

ABAP Language

  • Support of boolean expressions
  • Recursive references as enhancement of the ABAP Type System 
  • ABAP statement DELETE DUPLICATES without sorting

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • Signature comparison during CDS Migration from DDIC-based views to CDS view entities (see Signature Comparison and Migrating CDS DDIC-based Views to CDS View Entities)
  • Using bracket highlighting for ABAP CDS objects
  • WYSIWYG editor support for Review Booklet pages with two data providers (details)

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP)

  • Support of RAP Feature Control attributes readonly:create and mandatory:update (details)
  • Support of CDS Table Entities as draft persistence in RAP (details)

Analytics

  • Flexible Content Types for Review Booklets with Multiple Data Providers (details)
  • Enhanced App State Handling for Review Booklets (details)

Reuse Services

  • XCO Generation API for CDS Table Entities (details)
  • Maintain Translations app for the translation of email templates (details)

Business Configuration

  • You can now select Tree Table as table type in the SMBC object (details)

ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC)

  • Integration of ATC system in SAP BTP with ChaRM (details)
  • Improved ATC Check Results and Quick Fixes for SAP S/4HANA Custom Code Analysis 
  • New ATC Check Variant for Clean Core ABAP_CLEAN_CORE_READINESS to perform Clean Core analysis on SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA systems

Custom Code Analysis

  • Update accessible resources in Cloud Connector (see SAP Note 2861842 and SAP Note 3548772)
  • Manage Number of ATC Processes via Elastic Scaling (details)

SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • New app Manage News to author and manage news content that appears on the My Home page (details)
  • Reset Single Parameters in the Manage Launchpad Settings App (details)

Landscape Portal

  • Deploy Product: Install multiple add-ons in the same system (details)
  • New app Manage SAP System Upgrade (details)

Infrastructure

  • Transparent start time of the technical downtime for the next quartely upgrade (details)
  • Reduced minimum ABAP Runtime size with 0.5 ACU (details)
  • New Multi-Availability Zone Option with SAP HANA Cloud database configuration in a high-availability setup with a primary and a secondary instance in different availability zones (details)
  • Two new regions on Microsoft Azure: Toronto / Canada and Frankfurt / Germany (details)

entry.title: SAP Business AI in Life Sciences - Partner live expert session
entry.content:
SAP Business AI in Life Sciences - Partner live expert session

Life Sciences + AI = one of the fastest-moving conversations in the SAP ecosystem right now.

Customers want smarter operations, faster insights, and practical AI outcomes.  Partners who understand the industry context will stand out quickly.

Join our SAP Business AI in Life Sciences Partner live expert session and get:

  • Market trends shaping the industry
  • SAP Business AI use cases and references
  • Guidance on how to position AI with customers
  • Live demos in SAP Demo Environment 2.0
  • Q&A and tailored learning paths

Short session.  Practical content.  Real partner value.

Plus, seeing the demos live makes a big difference.  It turns abstract AI discussions into something customers can immediately picture inside their own business.

Seats fill quickly for these expert sessions.  Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/apr/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013941.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPBusinessAI #PartnerSuccess #EnterpriseAI #LifeSciences

entry.title: From XSUAA to AMS: Architecting S4HANA CAP Extensions with Policy-Based Authorization
entry.content:
Introduction

In modern SAP landscapes, extending S/4HANA is no longer just about building services or exposing APIs. The real complexity lies in how to secure enterprise extensions in a scalable and maintainable way.

Traditionally, SAP applications on SAP BTP have relied on XSUAA (XSUAA OAuth service) for authentication and authorization. While this model works well for scope-based access control, enterprise requirements often go beyond simple roles. They often involve business-context-based restrictions such as country, company code, or organizational unit.

As many of us, I have extensively used XSUAA to secure enterprise applications in SAP landscapes. In one of my recent projects, I had the opportunity to work with the IAS + AMS-based authentication and authorization model. That is when the WHY behind this architecture shift became clear.

As always, I wanted to share my learnings through a practical example - starting with the core concepts, followed by architectural differences and then a hands-on implementation.

The GOAL was not just to build another CAP application, but to understand a key architectural shift.

Let's get started by understanding the core design differences between the two authorization strategies

Core Architecture Differences

In SAP BTP - 
  • XSUAA has traditionally been the standard service for securing applications using scopes, role templates and role collections.
  • With AMS, the security model shifts toward a clearer separation between identity and authorization.

Let's start by understanding each model and key ideas behind it.

XSUAA Mental Model

Following diagram shows how the XSUAA model works in a simple way 

Figure-1

Key Ideas

  • IAS is the Identity Provider (IdP) responsible for authenticating users and establishing their identity.
  • XSUAA is the OAuth Authorization Server for the application, which issues JWT tokens containing scopes.
  • Authorization is scope-driven, where access control is enforced using scopes, role templates and role collections embedded inside the JWT token consumed by the application.

AMS Mental Model

Following diagram shows how the AMS model works in a simple way

Figure-2

 

Key Ideas

  • IAS is the Identity Provider (IdP) responsible for authenticating users and establishing identity.
  • AMS is the policy-based Authorization Engine that evaluates access rules based on roles, attributes and business conditions.
  • Authorization is policy-driven, where access is determined using configurable policies (e.g., country, company code) instead of static scopes.

Responsibility Shift

This comparison highlights a fundamental shift in how authorization is modeled: From static scopes defined at design time to dynamic policies evaluated at runtime.

AspectXSUAAAMS
Configuration filexs-security.json*.dcl
Authorization building blocksRoles + ScopesRoles + Policies + Attributes
Instance based data restrictionsLeads to role explosionStable roles + Dynamic policies
Change impactRedeploy neededPolicy update 
Runtime evaluationJWT scopesPolicy-driven (via ams plugin)

To make this architecture concrete, let’s walk through a simple vendor onboarding application that demonstrates how IAS, AMS and CAP work together in practice.

End-To-End Implementation: Hands-On

I have built a side-by-side extension - Vendor Onboarding application where:

  • Requesters can create and view vendor onboarding requests
  • Reviewers can approve or reject requests with comments
  • Role-based visibility is enforced using AMS policies to restrict data access

The following solution diagram illustrates the main components of the application

Figure-3

The above solution diagram illustrates the end-to-end architecture of the Vendor Onboarding application, including the CAP service, App Router, IAS authentication, AMS-based authorization and the SAP HANA persistence layer. It provides a high-level view of how the components interact in the overall system.

The complete GitHub repository for this implementation is publicly available and contains detailed documentation covering all aspects of the solution. In following sections, we will walk through some of the key AMS-related concepts and configurations used in this implementation.

AMS Authorization Policies

  • The cds add ams command generates an ams/dcl/ folder at the project root:
ams/dcl/
  schema.dcl                - AMS attribute schema (auto-generated; user-editable)
  cap/
    basePolicies.dcl        - auto-generated by cds build — DO NOT edit
  vendor/onboarding/
    reviewerPolicies.dcl    - hand-written app-based policies
  • Adding @restrict at the entity level is what triggers CAP's AMS plugin to generate basePolicies.dcl
@restrict: [
  { grant: ['READ', 'CREATE'], to: 'VendorRequester', where: 'requestedBy = $user' },
  { grant: ['READ', 'UPDATE', 'DELETE'], to: 'VendorReviewer' }
]
  • cds build translates this into
POLICY "VendorRequester" {
  ASSIGN ROLE "VendorRequester";
}

POLICY "VendorReviewer" {
  ASSIGN ROLE "VendorReviewer";
}

Instance Based Policies

To restrict VendorReviewers to specific countries, instance-based authorization is achieved by combining AMS schema attributes with policy definitions.
  • Defining AMS schema attributes: The first step is to expose relevant business fields as AMS attributes using CDS annotations. In this case, the country field is marked as an authorization attribute as shown below
@AMS.attributes: { country: (country) }
entity VendorRequests as projection on db.VendorRequests { ... }​
  • cds build then generates schema.dcl with: This registers country as a condition attribute usable in AMS policies.
SCHEMA {
  country: String
}
  • Write instance policies in ams/dcl/vendor/onboarding/reviewerPolicies.dcl:
POLICY "VendorReviewer_US" {
  ASSIGN ROLE "VendorReviewer" WHERE country = 'US';
}

POLICY "VendorReviewer_CA" {
  ASSIGN ROLE "VendorReviewer" WHERE country = 'CA';
}
 
This model enables fine-grained, instance-based authorization where the same CAP role (VendorReviewer) behaves differently depending on the policy context (e.g., country).

Instead of creating multiple roles per country, AMS allows authorization rules to be externalized into policies, improving scalability and maintainability.

The repository README contains the complete implementation details and configuration steps for this setup.

Wrap-Up

In this blog, we explored a practical implementation of a SAP CAP-based Vendor Onboarding extension secured using IAS and AMS and compared it with the traditional XSUAA-based security model.

The key objective was not just to build an application, but to understand the architectural shift in how enterprise authorization is modeled on SAP BTP.

With XSUAA, authorization is primarily scope-driven, where roles, role templates and role collections define access boundaries. While this model works well for standard role-based access control, it becomes rigid when enterprise requirements demand contextual restrictions such as country, organization, or cost center.

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my real world experience and learnings.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/ams
  2. https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-the-fundamentals-of-sap-system-security/introducing-sap-cloud-identity-services 
  3. https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/data-control-language-dcl

entry.title: Contextualize and reason Post-SAP Sapphire SAP Business Data Cloud briefing
entry.content:
AI pilots are easy.  Scaling trusted AI across the enterprise is where the real work starts.

Join our post-SAP Sapphire briefing for SAP Partners and learn how SAP Business Data Cloud powers the Autonomous Enterprise.

We’re covering:
• SAP BDC and Knowledge Graph
• Joule and Joule Studio
• AI Agent Hub and partner motions
• Faster migrations and governed AI
• New SELL, SERVICE, and BUILD opportunities

Plus, we’ll connect the technology to customer outcomes that matter: lower complexity, smarter automation, and faster decision-making across SAP and non-SAP systems.

One detail that stands out?  Customers are asking for AI they can trust, not disconnected copilots that create more noise.

This session helps you position the bigger story and turn it into action with your customers.

Register now and bring the Autonomous Enterprise conversation into every account.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/may/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014197.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPTechEd #AIAgents #SAPData #FutureOfEnterprise
